input.prompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

data.content:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-05T00:47:27.460Z</current_datetime>

I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to build a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server using stdio transport. They need:

1. A tool that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI)
2. A prompt template for summarization
3. Logging that doesn't interfere with stdio JSON-RPC protocol

Let me create a complete working solution. I'll set up a .NET project with the MCP SDK, configure stdio transport, add the tool and prompt, and ensure logging goes to stderr (not stdout, which would corrupt the JSON-RPC protocol).

Let me start by exploring what's available and then create the project.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-34a9031bf39646c4b9000f8005c4c243 && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null || echo \"dotnet not found\"", "description": "Check .NET SDK availability", "initial_wait": 10}

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-34a9031bf39646c4b9000f8005c4c243 && dotnet new console -n McpServer --force && cd McpServer && dotnet add package ModelContextProtocol --prerelease && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Http
arguments.description: Create project and add MCP packages

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Create project and add MCP packages

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-34a9031bf39646c4b9000f8005c4c243 && dotnet new console -n McpServer --force && cd McpServer && dotnet add package ModelContextProtocol --prerelease && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Http

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>net11.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="10.0.7" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Http" Version="10.0.7" />
    <PackageReference Include="ModelContextProtocol" Version="1.2.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
<exited with exit code 0>
